prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo beta-rich protein scaffold (60–80 residues) that preserves the active-site CXXC motif geometry of human thioredoxin-1 within a minimal non-thioredoxin fold for use as a redox nanotool. The designed scaffold should achieve equivalent or superior catalytic efficiency compared to wild-type TXN1. outline a methodology for: (1) backbone generation with RFdiffusion constrained to place catalytic cysteines, (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design with disulfide-bond enforcement, (3) ESMFold structure prediction and Rosetta enzyme design scoring. Provide guidance on redox assay validation strategies.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: Thioredoxin-1; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0
